basicDetails.schemeName: Financial Assistance to Students who Cleared Civil Services Prelims Conducted by UPSC/APSC

nodalDepartmentName.label: Tea Tribes and Adivasi Welfare Department

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme "Financial Assistance to Students who Cleared Civil Services Prelims Conducted by UPSC/APSC” was launched by the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Welfare Department, Government of Assam. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to students belonging to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community who have cleared the Civil Services Preliminary Examination conducted by the Union Public Service Commission or Assam Public Service Commission during the current year, so that they are supported in their preparation for the Mains Examination and succeed. The applications for this scheme are accepted online.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - Financial assistance to students belonging to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community who have cleared the Civil Services Preliminary Examination conducted by the Union Public Service Commission/Assam Public Service Commission during the current year. - The assistance is intended to support them in their preparation for the Mains Examination and succeed.
schemeContent.exclusions_md: - The applicants who have cleared both the Civil Services Preliminary Examinations Conducted by the Union Public Service Commission and Assam Public Service Commission in the same year shall be considered for scholarship only for the Union Public Service Commission Prelims. - The applicant should not be a Government service holder.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to students belonging to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community who have cleared the Civil Services Preliminary Examination. The benefits support their preparation for the Mains Examination to ensure their success.

children.text: The scheme "Financial Assistance to Students who Cleared Civil Services Prelims Conducted by UPSC/APSC” was launched by the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Welfare Department, Government of Assam. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to students belonging to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community who have cleared the Civil Services Preliminary Examination conducted by the Union Public Service Commission or Assam Public Service Commission during the current year, so that they are supported in their preparation for the Mains Examination and succeed. The applications for this scheme are accepted online.

Financial assistance to students belonging to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community who have cleared the Civil Services Preliminary Examination conducted by the Union Public Service Commission/Assam Public Service Commission during the current year.
The assistance is intended to support them in their preparation for the Mains Examination and succeed.

The applicants who have cleared both the Civil Services Preliminary Examinations Conducted by the Union Public Service Commission and Assam Public Service Commission in the same year shall be considered for scholarship only for the Union Public Service Commission Prelims.
The applicant should not be a Government service holder.
The applicant must first open the official Tea Tribes Welfare Department URL, www.ttwd.assam.gov.in, and then click the "Online Application" link to be redirected to the
New users must register themselves on the online portal, while existing users will log in using the username and password created during prior registration.
Once the applicant is logged into the Dashboard, they must select the appropriate scholarship scheme from the dropdown menu available.
The applicant proceeds to enter Personal Details where all required information in the application form must be accurately provided.
Location data, including District, Assembly Constituency, and Sub-Division, must be selected, along with other essential address details like Village/Town.
After filling in all necessary fields in this step, the applicant must click on the "Save Form Details" button to proceed further with the application.
Under the Upload Documents tab, the applicant must upload all required supporting documents in the specified image formats (e.g., JPG/JPEG).
All uploaded documents must adhere to specified maximum file sizes (e.g., photo maximum 50 KB, other documents generally 100 KB) before they can be saved.
The applicant must enter the Bank Account Number and the name of the bank under the "Update Bank Details" tab.
Details essential for electronic transfer, specifically the Bank Branch Name and the corresponding IFSC Code, must be accurately provided.
Once all bank details have been correctly entered, the applicant must click on the "Save Bank Details" button to confirm the information.
"Preview Application and Final Submission", displays all details entered in the form for a thorough and final check.
After cross-checking all information and uploaded documents for accuracy, the applicant must confirm the application by clicking the "Proceed with Submit Application" button.
Upon successful submission, an application number will be visible on the screen, allowing the applicant to view the status under the "APPLICATION STATUS" tab.

applicationProcess.process_md: ##### ***Step 1:*** ***Portal Access and Scheme Selection*** **A. Navigating to the Portal:** The applicant must first open the official Tea Tribes Welfare Department URL, www.ttwd.assam.gov.in, and then click the "Online Application" link to be redirected to the [SIRISH application portal](https://sirishassam.in/). **B. Initial Login or Registration:** New users must register themselves on the online portal, while existing users will log in using the username and password created during prior registration. **C. Selecting the Scheme:** Once the applicant is logged into the Dashboard, they must select the appropriate scholarship scheme from the dropdown menu available. ##### ***Step 2:********* **Entering Personal Details** **A. Inputting Mandatory Data:** The applicant proceeds to enter Personal Details where all required information in the application form must be accurately provided. **B. Specifying Location Details:** Location data, including District, Assembly Constituency, and Sub-Division, must be selected, along with other essential address details like Village/Town. **C. Saving Personal Information:** After filling in all necessary fields in this step, the applicant must click on the "Save Form Details" button to proceed further with the application. ##### ***Step 3:********* **Document Upload** **A. Uploading Required Files:** Under the Upload Documents tab, the applicant must upload all required supporting documents in the specified image formats (e.g., JPG/JPEG). **B. Document Formatting Check:** All uploaded documents must adhere to specified maximum file sizes (e.g., photo maximum 50 KB, other documents generally 100 KB) before they can be saved. ##### ***Step 4:********* **Updating Bank Details** **A. Providing Account Information:** The applicant must enter the Bank Account Number and the name of the bank under the "Update Bank Details" tab. **B. Entering Branch Identification:** Details essential for electronic transfer, specifically the Bank Branch Name and the corresponding IFSC Code, must be accurately provided. **C. Securing Financial Data:** Once all bank details have been correctly entered, the applicant must click on the "Save Bank Details" button to confirm the information. ##### ***Step 5:********* **Preview and Final Submission** **A. Application Review:** "Preview Application and Final Submission", displays all details entered in the form for a thorough and final check. **B. Final Confirmation:** After cross-checking all information and uploaded documents for accuracy, the applicant must confirm the application by clicking the "Proceed with Submit Application" button. **C. Tracking Status:** Upon successful submission, an application number will be visible on the screen, allowing the applicant to view the status under the "APPLICATION STATUS" tab.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: - The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam and must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community. - The applicant must produce a valid Caste certificate which is duly certified/issued by the District Commissioner of the concerned district or his/her designated officer. - The applicant should have cleared the Civil Services Preliminary Examination conducted by the Union Public Service Commission/Assam Public Service Commission during the current year. - The applicant must produce his/her copy of the admit card and result of the Civil Services Preliminary Examination conducted by the Union Public Service Commission/Assam Public Service Commission. - The applicant should not be a Government service holder.

children.text: The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam and must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community.
The applicant must produce a valid Caste certificate which is duly certified/issued by the District Commissioner of the concerned district or his/her designated officer.
The applicant should have cleared the Civil Services Preliminary Examination conducted by the Union Public Service Commission/Assam Public Service Commission during the current year.
The applicant must produce his/her copy of the admit card and result of the Civil Services Preliminary Examination conducted by the Union Public Service Commission/Assam Public Service Commission.
The applicant should not be a Government service holder.
